structures.name: Plurals
structures.description: Regular plural endings (-s, -es, -ies) and common irregular plurals.

structures.name: Imperatives
structures.description: Positive and negative commands using the base form of the verb.

structures.name: Basic Contractions
structures.description: Common contractions used in spoken and informal written English: I'm, What's, it's, isn't, Let's.

structures.name: to be (Present Simple)
structures.description: The verb 'to be' in affirmative, negative and question forms: am/is/are.

structures.name: Prepositions of Place
structures.description: Basic prepositions showing where something is: in, on, under, behind, next to, in front of.

structures.name: there is / there are
structures.nameDE: there is / there are – es gibt
structures.description: Using 'there is' for singular and 'there are' for plural to say that something exists.

structures.name: Subject Pronouns
structures.description: The personal pronouns used as the subject of a sentence: I, you, he, she, it, we, they.

structures.name: have got
structures.description: Expressing possession with 'have got' in affirmative, negative and question forms.

structures.name: Possessive 's
structures.description: Showing possession by adding 's to a name or noun: Tom's book, my sister's room.

structures.name: Adjectives with be
structures.description: Using adjectives after the verb 'be': Subject + be + adjective.

structures.name: because
structures.description: Using 'because' to give a reason: statement + because + reason (SVO word order).

structures.name: Days of the Week
structures.description: The seven days of the week, always capitalised in English, used with the preposition 'on'.

structures.name: Possessive Adjectives
structures.description: Words that show who something belongs to: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.

structures.name: can / can't
structures.description: Expressing ability and permission with 'can' and 'can't': can + base verb (no -s, no 'to').

structures.name: Present Simple (Affirmative)
structures.description: Describing habits and facts using the present simple affirmative: I/you/we/they + base verb, he/she/it + verb-s.

structures.name: a lot of / lots of
structures.nameDE: a lot of / lots of – viel(e)
structures.description: Using 'a lot of' and 'lots of' with countable and uncountable nouns to express a large quantity.

structures.name: Present Simple (Negative)
structures.description: Forming negative sentences in the present simple with don't / doesn't + base verb.

structures.name: Articles: a / an
structures.description: The indefinite articles 'a' (before consonant sounds) and 'an' (before vowel sounds).

structures.name: Adverbs of Frequency
structures.description: Frequency adverbs (always, usually, often, sometimes, never) and their position in the sentence.

structures.name: Present Simple (Questions)
structures.description: Forming yes/no questions and short answers in the present simple with Do/Does + subject + base verb.

structures.name: Object Pronouns
structures.description: Personal pronouns used as the object of a verb or after a preposition: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.

structures.name: Question Words
structures.description: Using question words (who, what, where, how often, why) to ask open questions.

structures.name: Frequency Expressions
structures.description: Time expressions for frequency: once a week, twice a day, three times a month, every day.

structures.name: Demonstratives: this/that/these/those
structures.description: Pointing words: this/these (near) and that/those (far), matching singular and plural.

structures.name: How much is / are …?
structures.nameDE: How much is / are …? – Wie viel kostet …?
structures.description: Asking about prices with 'How much is …?' (singular) and 'How much are …?' (plural).

structures.name: Present Continuous
structures.description: Actions happening now: am/is/are + verb-ing in affirmative, negative and question forms.

structures.name: Past Simple: was / were
structures.description: The past tense of 'be': was (I/he/she/it), were (you/we/they) in all forms.

structures.name: Ordinal Numbers
structures.description: Ordinal numbers (1st–31st) with spelling rules: most add -th, with exceptions for 1st, 2nd, 3rd.

structures.name: Prepositions of Time
structures.description: Prepositions used with time expressions: on (days/dates), in (months/seasons), at (times).

structures.name: Past Simple: Regular Verbs
structures.description: Forming the past simple of regular verbs by adding -ed, with spelling rules for -d, -ied, and doubled consonants.

structures.name: Linking Words: and, but, because
structures.description: Basic linking words to join ideas: and (addition), but (contrast), because (reason).

structures.name: Past Simple (Negative)
structures.description: Forming negative sentences in the past simple with didn't + base verb.

structures.name: Irregular Verbs (Past Simple)
structures.description: Common irregular verbs that do not follow the -ed pattern in the past simple.

structures.name: going to (Future)
structures.description: Talking about future plans and intentions with be + going to + base verb.

structures.name: Past Simple Questions
structures.description: Forming past simple questions with did + base form and was/were, including short answers.

structures.name: Irregular Verbs
structures.description: Additional irregular past simple forms: buy-bought, know-knew, make-made, write-wrote, expanding the M1 set.

structures.name: should / shouldn't
structures.description: Using should/shouldn't to give advice and make suggestions. No 3rd person -s, no 'to' after should.

structures.name: Comparatives
structures.description: Comparing two things: short adjectives + -er, long adjectives with 'more', and irregular forms (better, worse).

structures.name: Superlatives
structures.description: Expressing the highest degree: the + -est for short adjectives, the most for long adjectives, irregular forms (the best, the worst).

structures.name: as...as / not as...as
structures.description: Expressing equality (as...as) and inequality (not as...as) between two things.

structures.name: Prepositions of Place & Directions
structures.description: Expanded prepositions (opposite, beside, round the corner) combined with direction-giving language (go straight, turn left, cross).

structures.name: have to / don't have to
structures.description: Expressing necessity (have to) and absence of necessity (don't have to). 3rd person: has to / doesn't have to. CRITICAL: don't have to is NOT mustn't.

structures.name: might / might not
structures.description: Expressing possibility and uncertainty with might/might not. No 3rd person -s, no 'to'. Same form for all persons.

structures.name: Past Time Markers & Narrative Sequencing
structures.nameDE: Zeitangaben in der Vergangenheit und Erzählstruktur
structures.description: Using time markers (then, after that, finally, suddenly) and time expressions to sequence past narratives.

structures.name: some / any
structures.description: Using some in positive sentences and offers, any in negative sentences and questions.

structures.name: one / ones
structures.description: Using one (singular) and ones (plural) as pronoun substitutes to avoid repeating a noun.

structures.name: like + verb-ing
structures.description: Expressing enjoyment of activities using like/likes + verb-ing (gerund).

structures.name: must / mustn't
structures.description: must for strong obligation, mustn't for prohibition. CRITICAL: mustn't (nicht dürfen) is NOT the same as don't have to (nicht müssen).

structures.name: Possessive 's
structures.description: Expanded possessive 's including singular possessive ('s), plural possessive (s'), and irregular plural possessive (children's).

structures.name: Possessive Pronouns
structures.description: Possessive pronouns (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs) as standalone forms, contrasted with possessive adjectives.

structures.name: who / whose
structures.description: who for subject questions (no auxiliary needed) and whose for possession questions.

structures.name: Present Perfect (+ just)
structures.description: have/has + past participle for recent events with present relevance. Includes 'just' for very recent completion.

structures.name: will / won't (Future)
structures.description: will/won't + base form for spontaneous decisions, offers, promises, and predictions. Contrasted with going to for planned actions.

structures.name: Adverbs of Manner
structures.description: Forming adverbs from adjectives with -ly (slowly, carefully) and irregular adverbs (well, fast). Position: usually after the verb or object.

structures.name: Present Perfect: already, yet, ever, never
structures.nameDE: Present Perfect: already, yet, ever, never (Signalwörter)
structures.description: Present perfect signal words: already (completed, positive), yet (not completed / asking, negative/questions), ever (lifetime experience, questions), never (no experience, statements).

structures.name: So do I / Neither do I
structures.nameDE: So do I / Neither do I (Übereinstimmung)
structures.description: Expressing positive agreement (So do I / So have I) and negative agreement (Neither do I / Neither have I) with inverted word order.

structures.name: Past Continuous
structures.description: Describing ongoing past actions with was/were + -ing, including contrast with past simple for interrupted or simultaneous actions.

structures.name: Time Connectors
structures.description: Using when, before, after, while, during, until, and by the time to connect clauses and express time relationships in narratives.

structures.name: It takes ... to
structures.description: Expressing how long something takes using the construction It + take(s) + person + time + to + infinitive.

structures.name: Comparative and Superlative Intensifiers
structures.description: Intensifying comparisons with much/a lot/a bit/a little + comparative, and using (not) nearly as...as for nuanced comparisons.

structures.name: First Conditional
structures.description: Expressing real possibilities and likely outcomes with If + present simple, will + base verb.

structures.name: unless
structures.description: Using 'unless' as a synonym for 'if...not' in conditional sentences, always followed by an affirmative verb.

structures.name: Defining Relative Clauses
structures.description: Combining sentences using defining relative clauses with who (people), which (things/animals), that (both), and whose (possession).

structures.name: Present Perfect with for/since
structures.nameDE: Present Perfect mit for/since (Dauer und Zeitpunkt)
structures.description: Using the present perfect with 'for' (duration) and 'since' (starting point) to talk about actions and states continuing to the present.

structures.name: Past Simple vs Present Perfect
structures.nameDE: Simple Past vs Present Perfect (systematischer Vergleich)
structures.description: Systematically contrasting past simple (finished actions at a specific time) with present perfect (present relevance, no specific time, for/since).

structures.name: be (not) allowed to
structures.description: Expressing permission and prohibition using be (not) allowed to + verb across present, past, and future tenses.

structures.name: let / don't let
structures.description: Using let/don't let + object + base verb (without 'to') to express giving or withholding permission.

structures.name: could / was able to (Past Ability)
structures.nameDE: could / was able to (Fähigkeit in der Vergangenheit)
structures.description: Expressing past ability with could (general ability) and was/were able to (specific one-time achievement), including the full tense paradigm of be able to.

structures.name: Past and Future Permission (was allowed to / will be allowed to)
structures.nameDE: Erlaubnis in Vergangenheit und Zukunft (was allowed to / will be allowed to)
structures.description: Expressing permission across all tenses using was/were (not) allowed to (past), will (not) be allowed to (future), and have/has (not) been allowed to (present perfect).

structures.name: Future and Present Perfect Ability/Permission
structures.nameDE: Zukunft und Present Perfect bei Fähigkeit/Erlaubnis
structures.description: Completing the ability and permission system across all tenses: will be able to, will be allowed to, have been able to, have been allowed to.

structures.name: Present Perfect Continuous
structures.nameDE: Present Perfect Continuous (Verlaufsform des Present Perfect)
structures.description: Expressing ongoing activities with duration using have/has + been + -ing form, often with for/since, and contrasting with present perfect simple.

structures.name: Passive Voice (Present and Past Simple)
structures.description: Forming the present simple passive (am/is/are + past participle) and past simple passive (was/were + past participle), with optional agent (by).

structures.name: Second Conditional
structures.description: Expressing hypothetical, unlikely, or imaginary situations with If + past simple, would + base verb, and contrasting with the first conditional.

structures.name: Going to for Evidence-Based Predictions
structures.nameDE: Going to für Vorhersagen aufgrund von Beweisen
structures.description: Expanding the going to future to include evidence-based predictions alongside planned actions, and contrasting with will for opinions and spontaneous decisions.

structures.name: Past Continuous
structures.description: Deep revision of past continuous (was/were + -ing) for background actions, interrupted actions, and simultaneous past actions, contrasted with past simple.

structures.name: Past Perfect
structures.description: Past perfect (had + past participle) for an action completed before another past action, establishing temporal sequence in narratives.

structures.name: Reported Speech — Statements
structures.description: Reported/indirect speech for statements with systematic tense backshift, pronoun shifts, and time/place expression shifts.

structures.name: Reported Questions
structures.description: Reported/indirect questions: yes/no questions with if/whether and wh-questions with statement word order, no inversion, and tense backshift.

structures.name: Past Perfect with Time Connectors
structures.nameDE: Past Perfect mit Zeitkonnektoren (after, before, just)
structures.description: Extension of past perfect with time connectors after, before, just, and by the time, plus connecting ideas with because, although, however, in order to, so that.

structures.name: Adverbs of Manner
structures.nameDE: Adverbien der Art und Weise (Wiederholung + Erweiterung)
structures.description: Formation of adverbs from adjectives (adjective + -ly), irregular adverbs (well, fast, hard/hardly), and the linking verb rule (adjective after look, sound, feel, taste, smell).

structures.name: Question Tags
structures.description: Question tags with opposite polarity: positive sentence -> negative tag, negative sentence -> positive tag. Matching the auxiliary/modal or using do/does/did.

structures.name: Present Simple for Future (Timetables)
structures.nameDE: Present Simple für die Zukunft (Fahrplaune und feste Termine)
structures.description: Using present simple for timetabled or scheduled future events (transport, events, fixed schedules), contrasted with going to, will, and first conditional.

structures.name: want / tell / ask + object + to-infinitive
structures.description: The pattern want/tell/ask + object (person) + to + infinitive for expressing wishes, commands, and requests involving another person.

structures.name: Present Perfect vs Past Simple
structures.nameDE: Present Perfect vs Past Simple — Systematische Wiederholung
structures.description: Systematic review making explicit the connection between tenses and their time expressions: past simple with yesterday/ago/last, present perfect with ever/never/already/yet/just/for/since.

structures.name: Modals of Possibility: might / may / could
structures.nameDE: Modalverben der Möglichkeit: might / may / could
structures.description: Expanding the modal possibility system with might, may, and could for expressing different degrees of certainty. Includes the certainty scale: must > may/might/could > can't.

structures.name: Third Conditional
structures.description: Third conditional (If + past perfect, would have + past participle) for hypothetical past situations, combined with systematic revision of all three conditional types.

structures.name: Reflexive Pronouns
structures.description: Reflexive pronouns (myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves) for reflexive and emphatic use, including verbs that do NOT take reflexives in English.

structures.name: Phrasal Verbs
structures.description: Phrasal verbs (verb + particle combinations) with changed meanings, separable vs inseparable types, and the critical pronoun placement rule.

structures.name: Word Formation — Prefixes and Suffixes
structures.description: Word formation using negative prefixes (un-, in-, il-, im-, ir-, dis-, mis-) and suffixes (-ness, -ful, -less) to build new words and understand unfamiliar vocabulary.
